jp.title: 無類の勝負強さ健在…鎌田大地はECL決戦“ほぼフル稼働”、日本人初の欧州カップ2冠引っさげW杯へ
jp.description: 無類の勝負強さで日本人選手の歴史を切り拓いた。クリスタル・パレスMF鎌田大地は27日、UEFAカンファレンスリーグ決勝にボランチの一角で先発フル出場し、1-0の勝利に貢献。フランクフルト時代の2021-22シー...

jp.title: 指原莉乃「カード爆発するくらいお金使って」きっかけは＝LOVE 満島真之介が「素敵」と感激【CELEB SECRET】
jp.description: タレントの指原莉乃が、このほど都内で行われたABEMA新作のオリジナルドキュメンタリー番組『CELEB SECRET』（6月20日よる9時スタート）のMC囲み取材に、満島真之介、松井ケムリ（令和ロマン）、河野純喜（JO1）とともに出席。とっておきの秘密を明かした。

jp.title: 伊藤潤二原作オムニバスドラマの各話キャスト発表、村上虹郎・細田佳央太・真木よう子ら
jp.description: 伊藤潤二のホラーマンガ13作品を原作としたオムニバスドラマ「ストレンジ -伊藤潤二の夜も眠れぬ奇妙な話-」のキャストと各話ストーリーが一挙解禁された。

jp.title: 広く曇りや雨 北陸や北日本は落雷や急な強い雨に注意 今日5月28日(木)の天気
jp.description: 今日28日(木)は、曇りや雨の所が多いでしょう。北陸や東北、北海道では、午後は局地的に雷を伴って雨の降り方が強まりそうです。落雷や急な強い雨にご注意ください。全国的に湿度が高く、ムシムシするでしょう。

jp.title: 「阿部監督辞任ショックをベテランを先頭に立たせることで払拭した」巨人の橋上監督代行が指揮2戦目に坂本、丸らの起用的中で初勝利…ノムさん譲りのデータ野球の“神髄”見せる（RONSPO）
jp.description: 巨人が27日、東京ドームでのソフトバンク戦に5－1で逆転勝利をおさめ連敗を5でストップ、長女への暴行容疑で逮捕され辞任した阿部慎之助監督（47）に代わり指揮を執った橋上秀樹・監督代行（60）が2戦目

jp.title: 27日の米国市場ダイジェスト：米国株式市場は小幅高、ホルムズ海峡正常化期待が高まる(フィスコ)
jp.description:
■NY株式：米国株式市場は小幅高、ホルムズ海峡正常化期待が高まる
米国株式市場は小幅高。ダウ平均は182.60ドル高50644.28ドル、ナスダックは18.55ポイント安26674.73で取引を終了した。
ダウは終日堅調な値動き。ナスダックは前日終値...

jp.title: 「グロかった」「もっとやって」川村葉音被告（21）が裁判で証言【大学生集団暴行死】裁判官が「言ってることが理解できない」と異例の事態に 検察も「めちゃくちゃっすね」と女に指摘 強盗致死事件
jp.description: 北海道江別市で集団暴行を受け、大学生が死亡した強盗致死事件の裁判員裁判で、川村葉音被告（21）が、当時18歳の主犯格の男を「キレさせたら、私も暴力を振るわれると思っていた」と裁判で証言しました。

jp.title: やっとWindows 11のタスクバーが移動可能に。プレビュー版で新機能を検証
jp.description: すでに本誌でもニュースで紹介しているように、MicrosoftはWindows Insider ProgramのExperimentalチャネル、Preview Build 26300.8493以降において、Windows 11でのタスクバーの位置変更機能の展開を開始した。

jp.title: 「実質的には第三次世界大戦」 識者に聞くイラン・レバノン情勢
jp.description: 米国とイランによる戦闘終結のための交渉が続く中、レバノンではイランが支援するイスラム教シーア派組織ヒズボラとイスラエルの衝突が続いている。レバノンとイスラエルは米国の仲介で4月中旬に停戦合意を結んだものの、停戦は形骸化しつつある。今後の中東情勢はどうなるのか。レバノンの政治評論家、サルキース・アブ

jp.title: コンゴ「エボラ出血熱」再拡大…死者134人、WHOが緊急事態宣言 “封じ込めにくいウイルス”の謎【7つのポイント】
jp.description:
■感染疑い例536件・死者134人
　コンゴ民主共和国(以下、コンゴ)は5月15日、北東部イトゥリ州でエボラウイルス病(エボラ出血熱)の発生を正式に宣言した。
　5月17日には世界保健機関(WH

world.title: Canada introducing temporary Ebola-related border restrictions
world.description: The federal government is introducing temporary border restrictions and quarantine measures in response to an Ebola disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and growing concerns about spread in parts of East Africa. The Government of Canada announced Monday it plans to suspend several immigration documents for residents of countries considered at high or very high risk for Ebola outbreaks for a 90-day period beginning late Tuesday night. The temporary measures currently apply to residents of the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Uganda and South Sudan. Travel documents temporarily suspended Under the restrictions, individuals from those countries with previously approved temporary resident visas, electronic travel authorizations or permanent resident visas will not be permitted to travel to Canada while their immigration documents are suspended. googletag.cmd.push(function() { if($(document).width()<900) { s = googletag.defineSlot('/50748803/wco-all-bigbox', [300, 250], 'div-gpt-1632277500385-bbm1'); s.setTargeting('URL', [window.location.pathname]); s.setTargeting('position', [1632277500385]) s.setTargeting('story_id', 'canada-introducing-temporary-ebola-related-border-restrictions'); s.addService(googletag.pubads()); googletag.pubads().refresh([s], {changeCorrelator: false}); } }); The federal government also intends to temporarily pause processing new applications for those travel documents from residents of the affected countries during the same period. Officials said the measures are being implemented as a precaution while the international outbreak situation continues evolving. Mandatory quarantine measures begin Friday Additional quarantine requirements are scheduled to take effect May 30 and remain in place until Aug. 29. Under those rules, Canadian citizens, permanent residents, individuals registered under the Indian Act and foreign nationals who have been in affected regions within the previous 21 days and do not show symptoms will be required to quarantine for 21 days upon entering Canada. Travellers unable to safely isolate at home will be provided with an alternate quarantine location by the federal government. googletag.cmd.push(function() { if($(document).width()<900) { s = googletag.defineSlot('/50748803/wco-all-bigbox', [300, 250], 'div-gpt-1632277500385-bbm2'); s.setTargeting('URL', [window.location.pathname]); s.setTargeting('position', [1632277500385]) s.setTargeting('story_id', 'canada-introducing-temporary-ebola-related-border-restrictions'); s.addService(googletag.pubads()); googletag.pubads().refresh([s], {changeCorrelator: false}); } }); Anyone displaying symptoms associated with Ebola will be isolated in hospital for further medical assessment under measures authorized through the federal Quarantine Act. Federal government says risk remains low Federal officials stressed the overall risk to Canadians remains low. “There has never been a case of Ebola disease imported into Canada and there are currently no cases of Ebola disease in North America,” the Public Health Agency of Canada said in a release. Health Minister Marjorie Michel said the measures are intended to reduce the risk of the virus entering Canada while allowing officials to manage travellers according to their level of risk. “The health and safety of people in Canada is our top priority,” Michel said in a statement. “We will continue to take action guided by science and evidence to protect Canadians.” googletag.cmd.push(function() { if($(document).width()<900) { s = googletag.defineSlot('/50748803/wco-all-bigbox', [300, 250], 'div-gpt-1632277500385-bbm3'); s.setTargeting('URL', [window.location.pathname]); s.setTargeting('position', [1632277500385]) s.setTargeting('story_id', 'canada-introducing-temporary-ebola-related-border-restrictions'); s.addService(googletag.pubads()); googletag.pubads().refresh([s], {changeCorrelator: false}); } }); Immigration Minister Lena Metlege Diab said the government would continue monitoring the evolving international situation and adjust restrictions if necessary. Increased travel among concerns The federal government said the precautions are also being considered in the context of increased international travel connected to upcoming global events, including the FIFA World Cup. Officials noted travellers already inside Canada are not affected by the new restrictions and may remain in the country for their authorized stay period. Canadian citizens and permanent residents will still be permitted to return to Canada, but will undergo screening procedures at ports of entry. Ebola described as severe viral disease Ebola is a severe and often fatal viral disease spread through direct contact with bodily fluids from infected individuals or contaminated materials. googletag.cmd.push(function() { if($(document).width()<900) { s = googletag.defineSlot('/50748803/wco-all-bigbox', [300, 250], 'div-gpt-1632277500385-bbm4'); s.setTargeting('URL', [window.location.pathname]); s.setTargeting('position', [1632277500385]) s.setTargeting('story_id', 'canada-introducing-temporary-ebola-related-border-restrictions'); s.addService(googletag.pubads()); googletag.pubads().refresh([s], {changeCorrelator: false}); } }); Symptoms can include fever, fatigue, vomiting, bleeding and organ failure. Travellers are being encouraged to monitor federal travel advisories closely as border measures may change with limited notice.

world.title: When drug after drug failed, a teen hoped brain surgery could fix her daily seizures. Insurance denied it.
world.description: Several weeks after NBC News reached out to Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield about the denial, the insurer changed its policy to include coverage of deep brain stimulation for certain children.
